description:
You are acting as the on-call security engineer who has just finished running an automated credential-rotation job.  
  The job’s daemon writes its progress to one log:

  • /home/user/rotation/cred_rotation.log

  A compliance bot later ingests *only* the lines that show a **successful** rotation.  
  To feed the bot, create a new file

  • /home/user/rotation/successful_rotations.log

  that contains **exactly** every line from /home/user/rotation/cred_rotation.log which matches the following strict regular-expression pattern (notice the anchors and escaped brackets):

      ^\[[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\] Rotated credential for user .+ status=SUCCESS$

  Requirements the automated checker will enforce:

  1. Use a *single* terminal command (piping is allowed, but it must be executed as one compound command).
  2. The file /home/user/rotation/successful_rotations.log must  
     a. already exist when your command finishes,  
     b. be UTF-8 text with Unix line endings,  
     c. contain **only** the lines that match the regex above, in the **same order** they appear in the source log,  
     d. have **no extra blank lines** before, between, or after the matched lines.
  3. Do **not** modify /home/user/rotation/cred_rotation.log.
  4. Do **not** use sudo; you only have normal user privileges.

  Tip: standard tools such as grep ‑E, awk, or sed all understand the required regular expression.  
  After you run your command you can cat the new file to verify it looks right, but that verification step is optional.  
  Remember: the automated grader only inspects the existence and exact content of /home/user/rotation/successful_rotations.log.
truth:
<!--
    Initial file structure present **before** the agent begins.
  -->
  /home/user/rotation/               (directory, mode 755, owned by user)
  /home/user/rotation/cred_rotation.log  (file, mode 644, UTF-8)

  Contents of /home/user/rotation/cred_rotation.log (9 lines, each ends with '\n'):

  [2023-11-01 09:13:02] Rotated credential for user alice status=SUCCESS
  [2023-11-01 09:15:48] Rotated credential for user bob status=FAIL
  [2023-11-01 09:17:31] Rotated credential for user charlie status=SUCCESS
  [2023-11-01 09:18:20] Manual update for user dave
  [2023-11-02 10:22:11] Rotated credential for user dave status=SUCCESS
  [2023-11-02 10:25:46] Rotated credential for user eve status=FAIL
  [2023-11-02 10:29:50] Rotated credential for user frank status=SUCCESS
  [2023-11-02 10:35:59] Backup completed
  [2023-11-02 11:00:01] Rotated credential for user grace status=SUCCESS

  -------------------------------------------------------------
  Expected artefact *after* the agent completes the task
  -------------------------------------------------------------

  /home/user/rotation/successful_rotations.log  (file, mode 644, UTF-8)

  Exact contents of /home/user/rotation/successful_rotations.log (5 lines, each ends with '\n'):

  [2023-11-01 09:13:02] Rotated credential for user alice status=SUCCESS
  [2023-11-01 09:17:31] Rotated credential for user charlie status=SUCCESS
  [2023-11-02 10:22:11] Rotated credential for user dave status=SUCCESS
  [2023-11-02 10:29:50] Rotated credential for user frank status=SUCCESS
  [2023-11-02 11:00:01] Rotated credential for user grace status=SUCCESS

  The grader will verify:
    • the file exists,
    • it contains exactly the five lines above (no more, no less),
    • line order is preserved,
    • no extraneous whitespace or blank lines are present.
